title: Estimate via current-carrying capacity
description: Derive how many parallel vias a power connection needs from the via barrel size and the expected current.
body: Use when connecting a power net through vias between planes. Do not route high current through a single small via, since it can overheat and fail. Actions: estimate a single via's current capacity from its barrel diameter and plating, divide the total current by that figure, and verify the via count against a temperature-rise limit. Validation signals include a via count that keeps the temperature rise within the limit and a thermal scan showing no hot via. Risk boundary: an undersized via array overheats under load, so escalate to more vias or a wider barrel when the current margin is thin.
